description_long: This is a handheld megaphone with a classic conical horn design, featuring a wide, flared mouth at the front and a cylindrical body tapering toward the back. The handle is ergonomically shaped and attached beneath the main body, with a coiled cord connecting to a small microphone or control unit. The rear end of the megaphone has a visible battery compartment and a control panel, likely for power and volume adjustment. The surface appears to be made of molded plastic, with a smooth, slightly glossy finish. No visible branding or text is present on the exterior. The overall construction suggests it is lightweight and portable, suitable for use in public address or crowd control scenarios.
description: A handheld, conical megaphone with a wide flared mouth, ergonomic handle, and coiled cord microphone, made of smooth plastic. It is lightweight, portable, and lacks visible branding.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide, circular mouth of the megaphone and the central speaker opening.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the conical horn, ergonomic handle, and coiled cord attached to the microphone.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the battery compartment, control panel, and the attachment point of the coiled cord.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the full length of the horn, handle, and coiled cord, with no additional features visible.
attribute_mouthpiece_design: Circular, slightly protruding with a central cylindrical section and smooth contours, no visible covering.
